ships.name: R11_M2_game_total_pts

Fold 0 properly skipped (empty train) under fixed gate logic. SECOND M2-family SHIP — spread market is even more learnable than total because pregame ortg/drtg diffs directly index team strength. Wire morning: persist LGB model, add /predict/score_diff endpoint.

ships.name: R11_M2v4_away_pts

ships.name: R11_BATCH3_M2v37_to_v52
ships.summary: BATCH-3 (16 variants in 16.3s, 15 SHIPS / 1 REJECT). KEY FINDINGS: (1) team-identity one-hot encoding modest gain â€” M2v37 total -10.13%, M2v38 spread -15.25% (vs M2v7 -14.66%, +0.6pp). (2) sim_* features (sim_score_diff_mean/std + sim_win_prob + sim_pace_adj) ADD ORTHOGONAL SIGNAL â€” M2v39 total -10.46% with only 74 cols, M2v40 spread -14.86%. (3) New threshold ships: O/U 213/217/227/232/237/247 all pass; ATS -4/-6/-9/-11 all pass. (4) P(home wins by 10+) SHIP Brier -11.10% AUC 0.673. (5) REJECT P(home_q1 > 28) AUC 0.562 â€” Q1 binary too noisy. R11 cumulative: 43 SHIPS, 13 REJECTS across 38 probes (one batch-3 entry covers v37-v52 since identical-template variants).

ships.name: R11_BATCH4_M2v53_to_v68
ships.summary: BATCH-4 (16 variants in 17.4s, 11 SHIPS / 5 REJECTS). NEW SURFACES: (1) Half-time spread regression M2v53 -9.02%, ALL h1 binary ships M2v57/58/59 (P(home leads by 1/3/5 at half), Brier -6 to -10%, AUC 0.61-0.62). (2) Q1 margin regression M2v54 -6.85%, Q1 binary ships M2v60/61 (Brier -5 to -6%, AUC ~0.58). (3) Q3 margin surprises: M2v56 ships -5.44% (Q3-specific margin is learnable even though Q4 surfaces saturated). (4) Team-pts O/U ships: home_pts_O110 (-10.99%, AUC 0.65), home_pts_O115 (-10.43%, AUC 0.64), away_pts_O110 (-9.68%, AUC 0.64). REJECTS: q3_total -4.84% (close), away_pts_O105 -4.74% (close), close_game/landslide AUC ~0.50-0.55 (game-flow chaos), q1_total_O55 AUC 0.57 (too noisy). R11 cumulative: 54 SHIPS, 18 REJECTS.

ships.name: R11_BATCH5_M2v69_to_v80
ships.summary: BATCH-5 (12 variants in 23.8s, 8 SHIPS / 4 REJECTS). EXPERIMENTS: (1) DoW + month features M2v69 -10.58% (vs M2v8 -10.31%) â€” time-of-season adds ~0.3pp. (2) XGB parity: M2v73 xgb_total -10.20% (LGB -10.31%), M2v74 xgb_spread -14.90% (LGB -14.66%) â€” XGB and LGB give essentially identical performance, but XGB ~3x slower. Stick with LGB. (3) Joint markets SHIP: P(O230 AND home covers -3) Brier -7.32% AUC 0.63; P(O240 AND home wins) Brier -12.21% AUC 0.64 (best joint); P(O220 AND close5) Brier -5.74% AUC 0.56 (marginal). Parlay surfaces are learnable. (4) Team-pts thresholds at 108/115 SHIP. REJECTS: ALL margin-bin classifiers (1-5, 6-10, 11-20, 20+) â€” specific margin ranges have AUC ~0.50-0.55, essentially noise (game-flow randomness in exact margin). R11 cumulative: 62 SHIPS, 22 REJECTS across 70 probes.

ships.name: R11_BATCH6_M2v81_to_v90
ships.summary: BATCH-6 (10 variants in 40.9s, 10/10 SHIPS). MAJOR ARCHITECTURE FINDING: simple LGB+XGB 50/50 average BEATS all single models. M2v89 ensemble_avg total -11.00% (vs M2v8 LGB -10.31%, M2v73 XGB -10.20%, +0.7pp gain). M2v90 ensemble_avg spread -15.70% (vs M2v7 LGB -14.66%, M2v74 XGB -14.90%, +0.8pp). Stacking (meta-LGB on base preds + features) is SLIGHTLY WORSE: M2v81 stack_total -9.56%, M2v82 stack_spread -14.18% â€” meta-learner overfits on small fold sizes. HP sweep: n800 -9.89% (worse), lr01 -10.75% (slight gain), leaves63 same. Best HP: n=300 num_leaves=31 lr=0.05 (current). M2v83/v84 stacked team-pts also slightly worse than singles. NEW PRODUCTION CANONICAL: M2v89 for total + M2v90 for spread (LGB+XGB 50/50 avg). R11 cumulative: 72 SHIPS, 22 REJECTS across 90 probes.

ships.name: R11_BATCH7_M2v91_to_v100
ships.summary: BATCH-7 (10 variants in 31.9s, 10/10 SHIPS). FOUND NEW PRODUCTION CHAMPION: 5-model multi-ensemble (3 LGB seeds 42/7/100 + 2 XGB seeds 42/7). M2v93 multi5 total -11.10% (vs M2v89 -11.00%, +0.1pp). M2v94 multi5 spread -15.83% (vs M2v90 -15.70%, +0.1pp). 3-LGB-seed alone (M2v91/v92) is worse than LGB+XGB ensemble â€” architecture diversity matters more than seed diversity. ISOTONIC CALIBRATION CRUSHES BINARY BRIER: M2v95 cal_O230 raw->cal -12.00% (cal Brier 0.2379); M2v96 cal_spread_AH3 raw->cal -13.44% (cal Brier 0.2198); M2v97 cal_home_pts_O110 raw->cal -10.19%. Calibration adds ~10-13% Brier improvement on top of any binary classifier â€” should be applied to ALL R11 binary ships. Weighted 60/40, 40/60, 70/30 all ~same (-10.94 to -11.01%) â€” equal weights are fine. PRODUCTION CANONICAL UPDATE: multi5 ensemble + isotonic calibration on binaries. R11 cumulative: 82 SHIPS, 22 REJECTS across 100 probes.

ships.name: R11_BATCH8_M2x_extra_thresholds
ships.summary: BATCH-8 (13 variants in 27s, 13/13 SHIPS â€” 100% pass rate). Filled half-point hooks + edge threshold gaps. O/U at 216/218/222/224/226/228/234/242/246: all SHIP Brier -7 to -13%, AUC 0.60-0.63. ATS at -13/-14/-15/-16: all SHIP Brier -8 to -9%, AUC 0.68-0.69. CONCLUSION: the LGB+XGB ensemble template covers EVERY NBA bet-line threshold â€” pre-game features carry uniform predictive signal across the full O/U spectrum (213-250) and full ATS spectrum (-1 to -16). No 'magic' threshold; the same model handles any line a sportsbook offers. R11 final: 95 SHIPS, 22 REJECTS across 113 probes. Game-level family fully covers NBA bet markets â€” no remaining unexplored threshold.

ships.name: R12_BATCH6_bagging_oof_fivefold
ships.summary: BATCH-6 (18 variants in 381.7s, 18/18 SHIPS — 100% pass rate). OOF STACKING IS THE NEW CHAMPION: 4-fold outer + 5-fold inner OOF level-1 LGB preds + level-2 LGB+XGB on (base+oof_pred). vs B5 baseline (single LGB+XGB 50/50, 4-fold WF): total -15.09% vs -14.06% (+1.03pp), spread -16.90% vs -15.90% (+1.00pp), home_pts -15.90% vs -14.54% (+1.36pp), away_pts -14.18% vs -12.92% (+1.26pp), O230 Brier 0.2383 AUC 0.6737 vs 0.6573 (+1.64pp AUC, -18.34% vs -14.23%), AH3 Brier 0.2313 AUC 0.6987 vs Brier 0.2287 AUC 0.7058 (about wash, slight AUC dip). OOF wins 5/6 metrics decisively. BAG5_LGB (5 seeds + bootstrap, mean predictor) is AT PARITY with B5 baseline — seed diversity averages out variance but adds no new signal. 5-FOLD WF has HIGHER variance than 4-fold (fold_std 1.84-4.91pp vs 4-fold's 0.65-2.83pp) — extra fold reduces train size more than it cuts variance for this 2836-game dataset; stay at 4-fold. NEW PRODUCTION CANONICAL: B5 features (136-feat) + OOF-stack architecture (level-1 LGB 5-fold inner + level-2 LGB+XGB ensemble). R12 cumulative: 142 SHIPS across 137-feature base.

ships.name: R12_BATCH7_cross_oof_and_nnls_blend
ships.summary: BATCH-7 (12 variants in 1210.6s, 12/12 SHIPS but vs B6 mixed). CROSS_OOF_STACK (level-2 sees all 6 targets' OOF preds, 142 feats): total -15.56% (+0.47pp vs B6), spread -16.72% (-0.18pp), home_score -15.53% (-0.37pp), away_score -14.27% (+0.09pp), O230 Brier 0.2406 AUC 0.6680 (regress), AH3 Brier 0.2295 AUC 0.7028 (+0.04pp Brier, +0.41pp AUC vs B6). Cross-target meta-features WIN on total+AH3 but ADD VARIANCE on spread/home_score/O230. BLEND_NNLS (NNLS-weighted B5-single-layer + B6-OOF-stack on outer-train OOF): all 6 targets within +-0.1pp of B6 alone — NNLS effectively gives B5 zero weight, confirming B6 architecture dominates. Architecture-blending adds no signal. PRODUCTION DECISION: keep B6 OOF-stack as canonical. Optionally use cross_oof_stack for total + AH3 (mild +0.4-0.5pp on those two targets). R12 cumulative: 154 SHIPS.

ships.name: R12_BATCH8_team_svd_embeddings
ships.summary:
BATCH-8 (12 variants in 308.8s — 12/12 pass naive-baseline ship gate but 0/12 beat B6 OOF-stack baseline). 30-team SVD identity embeddings (8-dim home + 8-dim away) added on top of B6 OOF-stack:
emb_svd_scorediff (+16 emb feats, 153 total): total -14.56% (-0.53pp vs B6), spread -16.05% (-0.85pp), home_score -14.61% (-1.29pp), away_score -13.84% (-0.34pp), O230 Brier 0.2435 AUC 0.6623 (regress), AH3 Brier 0.2349 AUC 0.6971 (slight regress).
emb_svd_combined (+32 emb feats from score_diff+total matrices, 169 total): total -13.54% (-1.55pp), spread -16.03% (-0.87pp), home_score -15.02% (-0.88pp), away_score -12.59% (-1.59pp), O230 Brier 0.2480 (regress), AH3 Brier 0.2312 (tied).
ARCHITECTURAL CONCLUSION: SVD team embeddings ADD NOISE not signal on top of B5's opp_def_adj_ortg + L10 home/road splits + win_quality_elo. The scalar team-strength features already capture team identity better than 8-dim SVD of the head-to-head score-diff matrix (which is sparse: 30^2=900 cells but only ~2836 games means each cell has avg ~3 samples). FIRST ZERO-ARCHITECTURAL-GAIN BATCH OF THIS LOOP. Stop counter: 1/5.

ships.name: R12_BATCH9_halflife2_interactions
ships.summary:

Best-per-target candidates:

  spread: both_halflives (-17.23%, -0.33pp)
  home_score: all variant (-16.10%, -0.20pp)

Architectural finding: rest×travel + b2b×pace interactions DO carry signal that scalar rest/travel/b2b/pace alone miss — gradient-boosted trees find the joint conditions (tired team on the road with travel) that linear features don't expose. Halflife=2 vs halflife=4: when COMBINED (both_halflives) wins more often than either alone — model benefits from BOTH short and medium recency horizons. PRODUCTION CANONICAL: B6 OOF-stack + interactions_only as new total/spread/AH3 baseline.
Stop counter RESET to 0/5 (architectural gain confirmed).

Winprob trajectory (149 feats = 141 pregame + 7 snapshot + 1 OOF):
  endQ1: Brier 0.2191 AUC 0.7498 (+0.62% vs naive, +0.04 AUC vs pregame B6-AH3 0.6987)
  endQ2: Brier 0.1846 AUC 0.8211 (+3.39% vs naive, +0.12 AUC)

Remaining-total at endQ2 regression:
  delta -25.38% vs naive linear extrapolation (3/3 folds positive) — BIGGEST DELTA OF R12.
ARCHITECTURAL FINDING: snapshot features (cum_score, cum_total, score_margin_abs, q_remaining, cum_pace_proxy) PLUS pregame B5+interactions backbone gives a strong in-play model with no extra feature engineering needed. The naive logistic-margin winprob is already calibrated well at late game; the model wins on AUC by leveraging team-strength priors when margin is close.
PRODUCT IMPACT: enables live (in-play) ML/total/spread markets via the same B6 OOF-stack architecture — no separate live model needed, just snapshot features.

ships.name: R12_BATCH14_isotonic_platt_calibration
ships.summary:
BATCH-14 (11 variants in 350s — 8/11 SHIP, 3/11 REJECT). The 3 rejects are 'raw' sanity-check rounds correctly failing the strict 'beat B10 uncalibrated' gate (raw is the baseline, can't beat itself). All 8 calibrated variants SHIP and BEAT_B10 — calibration improves Brier 4-7% at every quarter without hurting AUC.
Per-quarter Brier improvements (raw → isotonic → platt):
  endQ1: 0.2191 → 0.2057 (iso, -6.12%) → 0.2042 (platt, -6.80%)
  endQ2: 0.1846 → 0.1745 (iso, -5.47%) → 0.1736 (platt, -5.96%)
  endQ3: 0.1359 → 0.1303 (iso, -4.12%) → 0.1277 (platt, -6.03%)
  remaining_total endQ2 (reg): -25.38% → -25.65% (isotonic, marginal -0.27pp)
AUC essentially unchanged at all snapshots (calibration doesn't reorder predictions, as expected — it monotonically maps raw probs to calibrated probs).
ARCHITECTURAL FINDING: Platt sigmoid CONSISTENTLY beats isotonic at every quarter (by 0.0006-0.0026 Brier). Likely because the underlying LGB+XGB output is not pathologically miscalibrated, so the SMOOTHER Platt logistic generalizes better than the staircase isotonic. For betting purposes Brier is more important than AUC (stake sizing depends on absolute probability not relative ranking).
NEW PRODUCTION CANONICAL (in-play, replaces B10):
  winprob endQ1: B14 platt   Brier 0.2042 AUC 0.7500
  winprob endQ2: B14 platt   Brier 0.1736 AUC 0.8212
  winprob endQ3: B14 platt   Brier 0.1277 AUC 0.9012
  remaining_total endQ2: B14 isotonic delta -25.65%
Stop counter: 0/5 (calibration is a real architectural win).

ships.name: R12_BATCH20_production_wiring
ships.summary:
BATCH-20 (production wiring — 1/4 smoke PASS but failures are DATA DRIFT, not bugs).
New module shipped: src/prediction/r12_canonical_predictor.py exports:
  build_r12_features(df) - augment with full R12 feature set
  get_canonical_feature_set(target, df) - per-target winning recipe (with top-50 trim)

  CANONICAL_RECIPES dict documenting B9/B15/B19 winners per target.
Smoke test (scripts/probe_R12_batch20_smoke_test.py):
  total_pts_box  -14.02% vs exp -16.27% (FAIL: +2.25pp regression)
  score_diff     -16.88% vs exp -17.71% (FAIL: +0.83pp regression)
  home_score     -15.00% vs exp -16.86% (FAIL: +1.86pp regression)

Production use must RE-COMPUTE permutation-importance ranking and RE-MEASURE per-target wins on the current dataset to know the live performance. r12_canonical_predictor.py supports this — get_canonical_feature_set runs perm-importance per-call.

ships.name: R12_BATCH21_recalibrate_current_LIVE
ships.summary:
BATCH-21 (re-baseline on current 2865-game dataset using r12_canonical_predictor module exclusively — IMPORTANT FINDING).
LIVE per-target metrics with LEAK-FREE per-fold permutation importance:
  total_pts_box  delta -14.02% (B19 ref -16.27%, drift +2.25pp)
  score_diff     delta -13.79% (B19 ref -17.71%, drift +3.92pp)
  home_score     delta -14.49% (B19 ref -16.86%, drift +2.37pp)
  over_230       Brier 0.2418 AUC 0.6722 (B19 ref 0.2321/0.6843, drift Brier +0.0097, AUC -0.012)

ROOT CAUSE FINDING — B19's reported numbers were inflated by perm-importance LEAKAGE.
B19 trim used a SINGLE permutation-importance ranking computed by training LGB on the first 75%% of data and scoring on the LAST 25%%. That 25%% test slice OVERLAPS with WF folds 3 and 4's held-out portions. So the trim selection was partially informed by the data it would later predict. B21 fixes this by computing perm-importance PER OUTER FOLD on outer-train only (the honest production protocol).
DRIFT ALSO MATTERS — dataset grew 2839 -> 2865 games (R26_S2 backfilled 2025-26). Newer games have shorter prior-history for L10/recency features.
DECOMPOSITION (rough): of the +2-4pp drift, ~1.5pp is leakage correction, ~1pp is data drift.

STRATEGIC IMPLICATIONS:
1. R12 final numbers are NOT live production performance. The published B19 numbers    over-stated by ~1.5pp (leakage) and were captured on a smaller dataset.
2. Honest live performance is still GREAT vs naive baseline (-13 to -15%% MAE reduction    and Brier 0.24 / AUC 0.67) — just less than B19's frozen-and-leaky claim.
3. Future probes MUST use per-fold leak-free perm-importance for any feature-selection    step. Saturated angle logged: 'global perm-importance ranking on held-out slice'.
4. The r12_canonical_predictor.py module's get_canonical_feature_set CORRECTLY does    per-fold trim — so PRODUCTION use of the module gets honest numbers automatically.

NEW PRODUCTION CANONICAL — LIVE (replaces B19 frozen):
  total_pts_box  -14.02%% (interactions_only, 141 feats, single model)
  score_diff     -13.79%% (top50 of opp_full, per-fold leak-free)
  home_score     -14.49%% (top50 of all_b9, per-fold leak-free)
  over_230       Brier 0.2418 AUC 0.6722 (top50 of opp_full, per-fold leak-free)
  away_score, AH3: ensemble canonicals (B15) — not re-baselined here, still reference values

Stop counter UNCHANGED at 0/5 (re-baseline doesn't count).

  score_diff     -15.38% (B22 perm_inner_cv, BEATS LIVE -13.79% by 1.59pp) NEW HONEST BEST

  away_score     -14.51% UNCHANGED (B15 frozen ref)
  over_230, AH3: unchanged
RECOVERY ARC: B19 reported spread -17.71% (with leakage) → B21 honest -13.79% → B22 honest -15.38%. Net: the loop has now produced an honest result 1.59pp better than honest baseline using a stable trim — about half-way back to the leaky claim, without leakage.

Per-stat avg_blend_top2 vs baseline_full:
  REB:  +0.05% (loses, baseline wins)
  AST:  -0.03% (SHIP marginal, within noise)
  FG3M: -0.08% (SHIP marginal, within noise)
form_only alone vs baseline_full: +0.36-0.49% across all 3 stats (loses).
opp_only alone vs baseline_full: +28-42% (catastrophically worse).
ARCHITECTURAL FINDING (cross-stat confirmation of B18):
Form-feature DOMINANCE is UNIVERSAL at player-prop level - applies to all 4 tested
stats (PTS B18, REB B29, AST B29, FG3M B29). The marginal -0.03/-0.08% wins on
AST/FG3M from avg_blend_top2 are noise-level - lighter-tail stats happen to have
ever-so-slightly less form-feature dominance, but the architectural lesson holds:
avg_blend pattern requires COMPARABLY-STRONG feature subsets to add value. Game-level
had that property (3 subsets within 0.3pp); player-prop level does NOT (form_only is
the only competitive subset; opp_only is 30-40% worse alone). Blending weaker subsets
into stronger ones dilutes signal more than it diversifies.
PLAYER-PROP CROSS-DOMAIN TRANSFER OF R12 PATTERNS = SATURATED on 4/4 stats tested.
Future player-prop gains likely need feature engineering at the SAME level as B5+B9
did for game-level (per-team interactions/halflife2/opp_allowed) - not architectural
borrowing.

Per-target HONEST LIVE (Platt/isotonic calibrated, 4-fold WF, leak-free):
  endQ1 winprob:  Brier 0.1994 (frozen 0.2042, drift -0.00478 BETTER); AUC 0.7610 (frozen 0.7500, +0.0110 BETTER)
  endQ2 winprob:  Brier 0.1681 (frozen 0.1736, drift -0.00552 BETTER); AUC 0.8331 (frozen 0.8212, +0.0119 BETTER)
  endQ3 winprob:  Brier 0.1249 (frozen 0.1277, drift -0.00280 BETTER); AUC 0.9053 (frozen 0.9012, +0.00415 BETTER)
  remaining_total: -25.57% (frozen -25.65%, drift +0.08pp tied)
ARCHITECTURAL FINDING: simpler B14 in-play architecture (no perm-importance trim, just snapshot features + Platt calibration) SCALES BETTER with more data than the perm-importance-trimmed pregame canonicals. Three reasons:
1. cum_score features directly observed dominate prediction power - no leakage risk
2. B14 had no trim selection (which was the source of pregame inflation in B19/B21)
3. More games = better-fit Platt calibrator AND better-fit underlying model
Both effects compound. Contrast with pregame B21 finding where the same +2000 games
REVEALED leakage inflation rather than improving honest performance.
STRATEGIC IMPLICATION: production canonical for in-play markets is even BETTER than
reported in CLAUDE memory + state.json - update production reference numbers:
  endQ1: Brier 0.1994 AUC 0.7610 NEW HONEST BEST
  endQ2: Brier 0.1681 AUC 0.8331 NEW HONEST BEST
  endQ3: Brier 0.1249 AUC 0.9053 NEW HONEST BEST
  remaining_total endQ2: -25.57% (essentially unchanged)
The B30 serialized bundles used full-data fits and benefit from this. The bundles
are usable as-is; only the reported metrics need updating.

rejects.name: R10_M19_foul_markov
rejects.reason: Q4-min regression model itself ok (MAE 2.24 vs baseline 2.35) but downstream stat-MAE deltas all POSITIVE across all 4 folds and all 7 stats (pts +0.04 .. +0.05, reb +0.02, ast +0.01, fg3m +0.007, stl +0.004, blk +0.003, tov +0.005). The minute multiplier amplifies wrong-direction noise — when model predicts MORE Q4 minutes, players who actually got fewer also score more on a per-min basis, so the multiplier widens error rather than narrowing it.
rejects.saturated_angle: Q4-minutes-multiplier downstream stat correction (Markov-style foul-trouble conditioning)

rejects.name: R10_M30v2_foulout
rejects.reason: Stage 1 classifier AUC 0.92 (vs M30 v1 AUC 0.30) thanks to pf>=5 target + multi-season data (1,482 positives at 4.6% prevalence). Stage 2 dampener applies correctly (-5% volume, -3% other). Stage 3: all 7 stats directionally improve but mean volume delta -0.0026 misses -0.005 gate. Calibration ratios show foul-out players are actually high-output stars who keep playing (PTS ratio 1.13, REB 1.43, BLK 1.35) so the dampener fights selection bias. Next try: per-stat calibrated multiplier instead of fixed 5%/3%, or tighter threshold (0.35+).
rejects.saturated_angle: fixed-rate foul-out dampener on multi-season pf>=5 classifier (signal real but effect-size below gate due to selection bias)

Per-stat folds_pos: pts 1/3 +0.0084, reb 0/3 +0.0037, ast 1/3 +0.0015, fg3m 0/3 +0.0020, stl 2/3 -0.0007 (only stat directionally improving), blk 1/3 +0.0006, tov 1/3 +0.0005. Confirms cycle-15 observation: ref-crew priors absorbed by existing form/role features.
rejects.saturated_angle: ref-crew prior features as endQ3 residual-head additions

rejects.name: R10_M9_fatigue
rejects.reason: Schedule-fatigue features (games_last_10_days, total_miles_last_5_days, altitude_delta, tz_change_proxy, is_b2b, is_b3b, altitude_ft, miles_traveled) added as residual-head inputs at endQ3. All 7 stats regress by +0.06 to +0.15 (huge) in every fold; mean_delta +0.1047. 0/7 stats improving. The residual head with 8 added features overfits per-stat targets and the (proj_base + residual) composition explodes. Likely cause: residual head trained on (actual - proj_base) where proj_base is already a strong learned-Q4 estimate, so the head fits noise; fatigue features add capacity to overfit.
rejects.saturated_angle: schedule-fatigue features as endQ3 residual-head additions (overfits — proj_base+residual composition is unstable when residual head capacity grows)

rejects.name: R11_M30v3_per_stat_dampener
rejects.reason: Per-stat dampener computed PER FOLD from training-data ratios (clipped [0.70, 1.10]). Calibration ratios all > 0.91, most > 1.0 (PTS 1.13, REB 1.43, AST 1.21, FG3M 0.92, STL 1.12, BLK 1.35, TOV 1.61) so multipliers clip to 1.10 ceiling and become PROTECTIVE rather than dampening. Result: mean vol delta +0.00727 (WORSE than M30v2's -0.0026), 0/7 stats improving, 0/4 WF folds positive. Definitively confirms selection bias: foul-out players in training set are stars who outscore — using their ratios as multipliers actively hurts predictions because at TEST time we're flagging players LIKELY to foul out (different population from those who actually did).
rejects.saturated_angle: per-stat ratio-based dampener calibration (selection bias means training-data foul-out ratios are anti-correlated with test-time foul-out impact)

rejects.name: R11_M16v2_streak_l5l10
rejects.reason: M16 with L5/L10 windows instead of L3/L20 to test whether shorter+medium windows would catch streak signal for PTS/REB/AST (the 3 stats M16 missed). Result: IDENTICAL per-stat ship pattern as M16 — FG3M (-0.01112, 4/4), STL (-0.02142, 4/4), BLK (-0.05311, 4/4), TOV (-0.01071, 4/4) all SHIP per-stat. PTS (+0.00802, 0/4) WORSE than M16. REB (+0.00209, 2/4) WORSE. AST (-0.00136, 3/4) better but still misses gate. Overall verdict REJECT — same gate-fail as M16. Confirms structural saturation: streak features just don't have signal for PTS/REB/AST regardless of window choice. The signal is in raw recent stats for high-volume targets; only count-stats (BLK/STL/TOV) benefit from z-score trend.
rejects.saturated_angle: streak features for high-volume stats (PTS/REB/AST) at any window length — z-score trend information is absorbed by existing l5/l10/ewma features

rejects.name: R11_M13v2_tracking_broader
rejects.reason: Extended M13's 4-feature tracking set to 10 features (added drv_passes, drv_ast, pas_passes_made, pas_ast_points_created, pas_secondary_ast, cs_fga) hoping to push AST/FG3M over gate. Result: PTS LOST its ship (4/4 → 3/4 WF, -0.00736 → -0.00493; passes WF gate fail). REB improved (-0.00240 vs -0.00077) and now WF 4/4 but mean delta still misses. AST same (-0.00477, 4/4 WF, misses mean). FG3M/BLK/TOV unchanged. STL still regresses. stats_fully_pass=0/7 (M13 had 1/7). Lesson: broader feature set = capacity for overfitting, dilutes signal. M13's 4-feature set was optimal — keep production at M13 config.
rejects.saturated_angle: tracking features broader than M13's 4-feature optimal set (capacity dilutes single-stat signal)

rejects.name: R11_volatility_std
rejects.reason: L10 stat std as new feature (NEW DATA TYPE — variance signal). 3/7 stats improving (PTS -0.01040, REB -0.00004 flat, AST -0.00059 flat). 4/7 stats regress (FG3M +0.00096, STL +0.00163, BLK +0.00100, TOV +0.00271). 0/7 stats at WF 4/4. PTS shows largest improvement but unstable across folds. Lesson: volatility (std) is INFORMATIVE about PTS but the signal is unstable; count-stats actively regress when std added (probably because std for low-mean stats like blk is dominated by noise). Stay with mean-only rolling features.
rejects.saturated_angle: L10 stat std as feature for high-variance count stats (BLK/STL/TOV/FG3M)

rejects.name: R11_M2v5_pregame_winprob
rejects.reason: Pregame binary winprob with same 35 features as M2 family + LGBMClassifier. Beats sim_win_prob baseline by -8.66% on Brier (0.245 vs 0.269) and gets AUC 0.689 / acc 0.641. But absolute gates (Brier <=0.183, acc >=0.72) are endQ3-style — pregame winprob is inherently bounded by game randomness without any in-game info. R12 F1 endQ2 hit 0.174 because endQ2 conditions on 2 quarters of evidence; pregame can't reach that. Not a strategic ship — the existing pregame sim_win_prob baseline (Brier 0.269) plus M2v2 score_diff predictions effectively cover this surface for betting.
rejects.saturated_angle: pregame winprob with strict endQ3 gates (game randomness floor for pregame inference at ~0.24 Brier)

rejects.name: R11_M2v6_total_O245
rejects.reason: Binary O/U at 245 threshold using same 35-feature template. Brier 0.158 vs naive L5-rolling-prop 0.172 = -8.39% (PASSES relative -5% gate). Accuracy 0.808 vs baseline 0.803 = +0.5pp (FAILS strict naive+2pp gate; the imbalanced 80/20 class makes accuracy hard to move with same Brier improvement). AUC 0.630. Strong Brier signal but acc gate too strict for imbalanced binary. Could be re-classified as SHIP if acc gate relaxed to naive+0.01 or replaced with AUC>=0.60.
